title: orcast tracked-limits register v1: ratified capability limits and risks for the submission window
statement: First-class, machine-authoritative register of orcast's known limits and residual risks at the submission window, reconciled against the current Vercel + App Runner topology (system_state_baseline_v3). Each limit is marked surmountable (a fix path exists and is owned) or unsurmountable for the submission window (a ratified hard limit, mostly scientific or scope). Seeded from the AX-9 dossier (.cca/AX_ADVERSARIAL_REGISTER.md), baseline v3 tracked_limits, the whitepaper pilot-scope and the model-card caveats, H0 risk assessment, and the Q/U gap registers. This register supersedes the free-text tracked_limits framing in the baselines as the canonical limit source; the docs and web surfaces draw from it so they cannot drift.

definitions.surmountable: A fix path exists and is owned; deferred only by the submission window or operator gate, not by a fundamental constraint.
definitions.unsurmountable_window: A ratified hard limit for the submission window, driven by data sparsity, scientific honesty, or product scope; not an infrastructure defect.

recently_closed: App Runner cold-start user-visible gap (closed by coldstart_mitigation_v1: MinSize=2 warm pool, measured gap = 0)
Self-host + Cloudflare primary path (retired by hosting_consolidation_v1 / selfhost_decommission_v1; App Runner is the sole backend)
Explore-session RDS write path (baseline v3: now reaches RDS, 200), resolving the prior DD-6 console impact
P0 authorization bypass on evidence/journal routers (require_api_key, d866e48; re-verified 401)

limits.statement: Acoustic detection rests on a single hydrophone station and detections are sparse in time and space.
limits.blast_radius: The acoustic signal feeding the forecast is thin; coverage gaps in space and time.
limits.disposition: Ratified hard limit for the window; honest on camera and on /gates.
limits.future_direction: Multi-station relay (OrcaHello array, ONC boundary nodes) graduated through the signal-modeling lane.

limits.statement: Acoustic detections are model confidence scores, not human-reviewed ground truth.
limits.blast_radius: A detection can be a false positive; confidence is not certainty.
limits.disposition: Ratified; labeled confidence score not ground truth on every surface that shows it.
limits.future_direction: Human acoustic review loop feeding a labeled detection set.

limits.statement: Community, OBIS, and iNaturalist sightings are effort-biased.
limits.blast_radius: Apparent hotspots can reflect observer effort rather than whale density.
limits.disposition: Ratified; the fitness gates downweight and the model card states it.
limits.future_direction: Effort-correction covariate (AIS effort, detectability) from the signal-modeling graduation research.

limits.statement: The fitness gates currently decline to promote high confidence; the public forecast shows 0% promoted confidence in the pilot.
limits.blast_radius: The map shows an honest 0% rather than a falsely precise number.
limits.disposition: Ratified by design; this is the negative-deviance skill, shown with its gate caveat and real number.
limits.future_direction: More data and graduated covariates may let the gates earn a promotion decision.

limits.statement: The forecast is scoped to the San Juan and Salish core pilot region.
limits.blast_radius: Cells outside the pilot region are honest nulls, not predictions.
limits.disposition: Ratified; provenance says honest outside the pilot region.
limits.future_direction: Expand the forecast grid as coverage and validation allow.

limits.statement: Several covariates (SST front, currents, prey) are excluded from the current kernel.
limits.blast_radius: The forecast is grounded on a reduced covariate set.
limits.disposition: Tracked through the MLOps signal-modeling graduation lane.
limits.future_direction: Graduate covariates (TA/TB research) into the kernel with gate validation.

limits.statement: The terrain and bathymetry 3D twin is modeled in the research sandbox, not a shipped production research route; modeled not measured.
limits.blast_radius: The twin is a modeling surface, not a delivered feature in the submission window.
limits.disposition: Ratified; framed as modeled and as direction in the submission.
limits.future_direction: Ship the twin as a research route with geo-anchored labels and the datum/scale fixes already landed.

limits.statement: dtag modeling replay and a full collaborative-annotation workbench are direction, not shipped; hydrophone-detection annotation and the moderation review run today.
limits.blast_radius: The B-side workbench is partially live (review, annotation) and partially direction.
limits.disposition: Ratified; framed honestly as direction in the submission.
limits.future_direction: Build dtag replay and multi-user annotation on the existing moderation and annotation seams.

limits.statement: The pax-Friend liquid-glass orchestration console (LGC) is chartered, not shipped.
limits.blast_radius: The AI orchestration bridge is named as direction, not a delivered surface.
limits.future_direction: Build the liquid-glass console per LIQUID_GLASS_CONSOLE_MANIFEST.md.

limits.statement: SES is in sandbox; interest email only sends to verified recipients (inline link delivery works for everyone).
limits.blast_radius: Interest email to an unverified public signup does not send.
limits.disposition: Mitigate via an SES production-access request (operator AWS ticket).
limits.future_direction: SES production access removes the verified-recipient restriction.

limits.statement: App Runner is the sole live backend; the self-host rollback is dormant with a degraded RDS path, so there is no clean warm fallback.
limits.blast_radius: A region or service failure has no instant rollback target.
limits.disposition: Accept for the window; the cold-start gap itself is closed (MinSize=2 warm pool, measured gap = 0).
limits.future_direction: Repair the self-host RDS path or stand up a second-region App Runner.

limits.statement: The self-host service and Cloudflare route are decommissioned, but the EC2 checkout and host IAM are left inert; physical teardown is deferred to the operator post-submission.
limits.blast_radius: Inert artifacts on a shared host; the pax co-tenants are preserved.
limits.future_direction: Terminate the inert orcast checkout and host IAM when convenient.

limits.statement: ONC hydrophone integration is disabled in production (metadata-only, 503 before any fetch).
limits.blast_radius: The hydrophone panel shows metadata only.
limits.disposition: Enable on demand after the ONC archive-file filename SSRF validation (AX-2).
limits.future_direction: Enable ONC with input validation on the archive-file path.

limits.statement: The explore3d live R3F twin frontend is not deployed to production; only the sandbox routes exist.
limits.blast_radius: The 3D twin is not reachable from the production nav.
limits.disposition: Decision pending; tracked through the TWIN lane.
limits.future_direction: Deploy the twin route once labels, scaling, and LGC styling land.

limits.statement: Host access is SSM-only, with no SSH fallback.
limits.blast_radius: Host access depends on SSM availability.
limits.disposition: Accept; more secure than an open SSH port.
limits.future_direction: Re-add SSH via the API only if SSM degrades.

limits.statement: SDR O-2/O-3/O-4 internal gate reds and claim drift remain open.
limits.blast_radius: Internal gate reds; no judge-facing claim affected.
limits.future_direction: Close the SDR drift items as the lanes that own them land.

limits.statement: Recorded demo narration is not prose-gated and the demo gallery GIFs are open.
limits.blast_radius: Spoken narration may carry phrasing the text prose gate would block; gallery placeholders.
limits.disposition: Addressed by the new director-based demo production lane (DEMO-PROD) with the R3/R5 honesty rubric on every captured beat.
limits.future_direction: Render the new demo from approved takes only; export the gallery GIFs from the same recordings.

non_claims: This register does not change any model output, gate verdict, or topology; it records limits and risks, it does not fix them.
An unsurmountable-window limit is a ratified scope or scientific-honesty limit, not an admission of an infrastructure defect.
The recently_closed list records limits resolved by prior ratified decisions; it is not a claim that no new limits will appear.
